verdict.answer_first: B states the answer immediately: "lets a third-party app get access...without ever seeing their password"; A also does, but after a title and broader framing.
verdict.decisiveness: B is more direct and focused; it commits to the flow and key mistakes, while A ends by handing off to another possible example: "Want me to show..."
verdict.fabrication: A has the least grounded precise claim: "OAuth 2.1 makes it mandatory" without evidence; B's "RFC 7636" at least anchors PKCE.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because the steps are shorter, ordered, and end with the two common implementation mistakes: "validating state" and "treating the authorization code as if it were the token".
